title: Tens of thousands rally at Boston Common for third ‘No Kings’ protest
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Tens of thousands attended the 'No Kings' protest at Boston Common, as part of a nationwide demonstration.
Organized by groups including ACLU of Massachusetts, the event featured speeches, performances, and 'action tables' for advocacy sign-ups.
Speakers like Gov. Maura Healey and Sen. Elizabeth Warren addressed topics from political threats to military involvement and immigration.

title: Review & setlist: GWAR gives fanbase a bloody good time at House of Blues
author: Christopher Hurley
bullets: GWAR performed a 90-minute set at Boston's House of Blues, featuring 17 songs and theatrical bloody antics.
The band played songs like 'Tormentor' and 'Hail, Genocide!', while drenching the audience in fake blood.
GWAR's performance included satirical elements targeting political figures and humorous on-stage violence.

title: Celtics post lengthy injury report ahead of Sunday’s Hornets matchup
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jaylen Brown and Jayson Tatum among four Celtics listed as questionable for Sunday's game against the Hornets.
Brown is dealing with left Achilles tendinitis, while Tatum is managing right Achilles repair.
Nikola Vucevic is confirmed out with a right ring finger fracture for the Celtics' matchup with Charlotte.

title: ‘No Kings’ protest: What protesters said and why they showed up
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Thousands gathered on Boston Common for the 'No Kings' protest, part of over 160 rallies in Massachusetts.
Prominent speakers included Gov. Maura Healey, Sen. Ed Markey, Sen. Elizabeth Warren, and performances by Dropkick Murphys.
Protesters expressed concerns over the country's direction, with some using costumes to emphasize the anti-monarchy theme.

title: Photo scroll: Scenes from Boston’s ‘No Kings’ protest on March 28
author: Emily Turner
bullets: Thousands gathered at Boston Common for the 'No Kings' protest on March 28, part of a national movement.
Governor Maura Healey and Senators Edward Markey and Elizabeth Warren addressed the protestors in Boston.
Over 160 'No Kings' events took place across Massachusetts, highlighting widespread participation in the protest.

title: Photos: ‘No Kings’ sparks protests in Europe
author: Boston.com Staff
bullets: 'No Kings' protests against Donald Trump occur in Madrid, Paris, Rome, and Amsterdam on March 28, 2026.
'No Kings' emerges from the U.S. as a major opposition against Trump's second term beginning January 2025.
More than 3,000 'No Kings' protests planned across the U.S., Massachusetts hosting second-most events after California.

title: Bravo Just Dropped the first full “Real Housewives of Rhode Island” Trailer. Here’s everything we learned.
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: 'The Real Housewives of Rhode Island' premieres April 2 at 9 p.m. on Bravo, the first New England Housewives series.
The trailer features Alicia Carmody, a Providence restaurant owner, and Liz McGraw, a cannabis dispensary owner from Cranston.
RHORI explores Rhode Island's social scene, with themes of friendships, feuds, and shifting loyalties within close-knit communities.

title: Hampden County Sheriff plans dozens of job cuts
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Hampden County Sheriff's Department to cut 50 positions to save up to $4 million amid budget cuts.
Reduction affects police presence in Springfield, West Springfield, Holyoke, Palmer; specialist units will be reduced.
Cuts result from statewide funding issues, not overspending, dating back to county government restructuring in 1999.

title: Quincy approves money for new firefighting gear after PFAS found in old gear
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Quincy city council approved $2.65 million to replace firefighters' gear due to PFAS contamination concerns.
New PFAS-free gear will be purchased for over 270 firefighters following safety and health debates.
PFAS exposure linked to cancer; Massachusetts law will ban gear with PFAS starting January 2025.

title: With his new doc  ‘Henry David Thoreau,’ Don Henley returns to Walden
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Don Henley, Eagles co-founder, executive produced a 'Henry David Thoreau' documentary airing on PBS March 30-31.
Henley's Walden Woods Project, established in 1990, preserves Thoreau's legacy and environmental stewardship values.
The documentary features narrations by George Clooney and voices by Jeff Goldblum, Meryl Streep, and Ted Danson.
